engineIntelligence.h2: Renault 19 Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Renault 19 in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Renault 19 E7J engine - petrol 1.4L replacement UK
knownFailures: Head gasket failure - common on high-mileage units, causes coolant loss and overheating.
Timing belt wear - replacement required every 60k miles to prevent valve damage.

cards.altText: Renault 19 F8Q engine - diesel 1.9L replacement UK
knownFailures: Injection pump failure - causes hard starting, loss of power, or engine cut-out on F8Q diesels.
Glow plug circuit issues - common cold-start difficulty on pre-1993 units.

cards.altText: Renault 19 F7P engine - petrol 1.8L 16V replacement UK
knownFailures: Cylinder head warping - high-revving 16V design prone to head gasket failure under stress.
Timing belt tensioner wear - critical to replace every 60k miles to avoid catastrophic valve damage.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,800-£2,800 (supply only) Estimated from comparable F7P units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Renault 19 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Renault 19 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: The F8Q powered all UK Renault 19 diesel variants, offering strong economy but prone to injection pump wear on high-mileage examples.
compatibleVariants: 1.9 D, 1.9 TD, Chamade 1.9 D, Cabriolet 1.9 TD
commonFailures: Injection pump failure - causes hard starting, power loss, or cut-out; documented on units over 120k miles.
Glow plug circuit faults - common cold-start difficulty on pre-1993 F8Q engines.
Turbo wastegate sticking (TD variants) - reduces boost pressure and acceleration.
entries.cta: Get quotes for F8Q Renault 19 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: The E7J replaced older Cléon-Fonte units in Phase 2 models, bringing fuel injection and improved refinement to the 1.4L range.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure - common on high-mileage E7J units, causes coolant loss and overheating.
Timing belt wear - replacement required every 60k miles to prevent valve damage.
Throttle body carbon buildup - causes hesitation on city-driven examples.
entries.cta: Get quotes for E7J Renault 19 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: Early Phase 1 1.4L engine using single-point injection, found in base RN and RT models before the E7J update.
commonFailures: Carburettor/injection transition issues - early SPI units prone to fuel delivery inconsistencies.
Valve stem seal wear - increases oil consumption on units over 100k miles.
entries.cta: Get quotes for C3J Renault 19 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.7L Petrol
entries.history: The versatile F3N powered mid-to-high trim 19s, offering a balance of performance and reliability across carburettor and injection variants.
commonFailures: Timing belt tensioner failure - belt slip on high-mileage units if service intervals are missed.
Cylinder head warping - documented on pre-1992 F3N units under sustained load.
EGR valve clogging (injection variants) - causes rough idle and reduced power.
entries.cta: Get quotes for F3N Renault 19 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: The refined 1.8L F3P replaced the 1.7L in Phase 2 models, offering smoother power delivery for luxury trims like Baccara.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure - common on high-mileage F3P units, particularly with missed coolant changes.
Water pump failure - leads to overheating if not replaced with timing belt service.
entries.cta: Get quotes for F3P Renault 19 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L 16V Petrol
entries.history: The high-revving F7P 16V powered the coveted 16S/16V models, delivering sports-car performance but requiring meticulous maintenance.
commonFailures: Cylinder head warping - high-revving design prone to head gasket failure under stress or overheating.
Timing belt tensioner wear - critical to replace every 60k miles to avoid catastrophic valve damage.
Hydraulic lifter noise - common on high-mileage F7P units, indicates valve train wear.
entries.cta: Get quotes for F7P Renault 19 engine replacement

engineGuide.closing: Can't find your engine code? Enter your registration number above - we'll instantly identify the exact unit, its technical specifications, and live replacement prices from UK Renault 19 engine specialists. Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Renault 19 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Renault 19's E7J, F3N and F8Q engines dominate UK replacement enquiries, with head gasket failure and injection pump issues driving most searches. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Head Gasket Failure - Why Renault 19 Engines Overheat and Fail
problems.affectedModels: Renault 19 RN, RT, TXE, GTX (1989-1995, E7J, F3N, F3P petrol engines)
problems.rootCause: Cylinder head warping due to inadequate cooling system maintenance, causing gasket breach between coolant and combustion chambers.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace gasket, machine head if warped, reassemble with new bolts.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if block is undamaged; risk of repeat failure if root cause not addressed.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full head strip, valve grind, new guides/seals, pressure test, reassemble with upgraded gasket.
repairOptions.longevity: More reliable than basic gasket swap; suitable for engines under 100k miles with good block condition.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of fully rebuilt engine with upgraded head, new timing components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage or severely damaged units; eliminates repeat failure risk.

problems.recommendation: On a Renault 19 worth £1,500-£3,000, a full rebuilt engine often offers better long-term value than repeated head repairs. We recommend replacement if the block shows signs of warping or if the car has exceeded 100k miles.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt E7J/F3N engine prices for your Renault 19

problems.h4: Injection Pump Failure - Why Renault 19 Diesel Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Renault 19 1.9 D, 1.9 TD (1989-1995, F8Q diesel engines)
problems.rootCause: Wear in the rotary injection pump's internal components, leading to poor fuel delivery, hard starting, or complete failure to start.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove pump, bench-test, replace worn internal components, recalibrate timing and delivery.
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for early-stage wear; may not resolve severe internal damage or high-mileage units.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of fully rebuilt F8Q engine with new/reconditioned injection pump, bench-tested, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Best solution for pumps with internal scoring or high-mileage engines; eliminates repeat failure risk.

problems.recommendation: For F8Q engines over 120k miles, replacing the entire engine with a rebuilt unit is often more cost-effective than pump repair alone, especially if other wear items are present.
problems.cta: Get quotes for F8Q Renault 19 diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Why Renault 19 Engines Suffer Catastrophic Damage
problems.affectedModels: Renault 19 all petrol variants (1988-1995, E7J, F3N, F3P, F7P engines)
problems.rootCause: Timing belt wear or tensioner failure causing belt jump or breakage, resulting in piston-to-valve contact on interference engines.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idlers, and water pump; reset timing marks to factory specs.
repairOptions.longevity: Essential preventative maintenance; must be done every 60k miles to avoid catastrophic failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of fully rebuilt engine after belt failure damage; includes new valves, pistons if needed, bench-tested, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Only option after catastrophic interference damage; rebuilt units include upgraded components to prevent repeat issues.

problems.recommendation: Never skip the 60k-mile timing belt service on Renault 19 petrol engines. If belt failure has occurred, a rebuilt engine is the only reliable solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt F3N/F7P engine prices for your Renault 19

problems.h4: Oil Leak and Consumption - Why Renault 19 Engines Burn Oil
problems.affectedModels: Renault 19 all variants (1988-1995, all engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Wear in valve stem seals, piston rings, or rear main seal, allowing oil to enter combustion chambers or leak externally.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace valve stem seals, camshaft seals, or rear main seal; clean oil passages.
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for moderate oil consumption; may not resolve severe ring wear or block scoring.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of fully rebuilt engine with new rings, bearings, seals; bench-tested, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Best solution for engines with high oil consumption or visible smoke; eliminates repeat leak risk.

problems.recommendation: If oil consumption exceeds 1 litre per 1,000 miles or blue smoke is visible, a rebuilt engine offers better long-term reliability than repeated seal replacements.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt Renault 19 engines with upgraded seals and rings

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Renault 19
finalCta.paragraph: From head gasket failures on E7J petrol units to injection pump wear on F8Q diesels, Renault 19 engines face well-documented challenges. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Renault 19 specialists for rebuilt, reconditioned or used replacement engines. Every unit includes upgraded components addressing original failure points, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Renault 19 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Renault 19 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Renault 19 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Renault 19 is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Renault 19 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.9L F8Q diesel was the sole diesel option for the Renault 19 in the UK, offered in naturally aspirated (D) and turbocharged (TD) forms across hatchback, saloon and convertible body styles from 1989 to 1995. Known for strong fuel economy but vulnerable to injection pump wear on high-mileage examples, this engine remains popular with high-mileage drivers seeking economical replacement options.
commonFailurePoints: Injection pump failure causing hard starting or power loss; glow plug circuit faults on pre-1993 units
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault 19 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Renault 19 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The smallest petrol option for the Renault 19, the 1.2L C1G and E7F engines powered base RN models in early production years. These OHV and SOHC units offered modest performance but are now rare in the UK market due to low survival rates and limited replacement demand.
commonFailurePoints: Carburettor/injection transition issues on early SPI units; valve stem seal wear increasing oil consumption

items.title: Renault 19 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The most common petrol displacement for the Renault 19, the 1.4L C3J and E7J engines powered RN, RT, TXE and Alize variants across both Phase 1 and Phase 2 production. The E7J's fuel injection brought improved refinement over the earlier C3J, though both suffer from head gasket vulnerability on high-mileage examples.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure causing coolant loss; timing belt wear requiring 60k-mile replacement intervals

items.title: Renault 19 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L C2L and C3L engines offered a mid-range power option for RN and RT variants in later production years. These OHV units provided stronger torque than the 1.4L but are less common in the UK replacement market due to lower original fitment numbers.
commonFailurePoints: Valve train wear on high-mileage units; cooling system corrosion leading to overheating

items.title: Renault 19 1.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The versatile 1.7L F2N and F3N engines powered TS, GTX, TXE and Chamade variants across carburettor, single-point and multi-point injection configurations. Offering a balance of performance and economy, these engines dominate mid-range replacement enquiries but require careful timing belt maintenance to avoid catastrophic failure.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt tensioner failure causing valve damage; cylinder head warping on pre-1992 units under load

items.title: Renault 19 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The refined 1.8L F3P engine replaced the 1.7L in Phase 2 luxury trims like Baccara and Executive, offering smoother power delivery and improved refinement. While less prone to head gasket issues than earlier F-series units, the F3P still requires diligent cooling system maintenance to ensure longevity.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure on high-mileage units; water pump failure leading to overheating if not serviced with timing belt

items.title: Renault 19 1.8L 16V Petrol Engines
items.description: The high-performance 1.8L F7P 16-valve engine powered the coveted 16S and 16V models, delivering sports-car acceleration but demanding meticulous maintenance. With only around 140hp from a naturally aspirated 1.8L block, this engine rewards careful owners but punishes neglect with costly head gasket or timing belt failures.
commonFailurePoints: Cylinder head warping under high-rev use; timing belt tensioner wear requiring strict 60k-mile replacement

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Renault 19 needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Renault 19 specialists.
headingLines: Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant

knownFor: Strong aftermarket parts availability for common codes like E7J and F3N
Head gasket failure on high-mileage E7J/F3N units without coolant maintenance
Timing belt criticality - interference design means failure causes valve damage
F7P 16V rewards meticulous maintenance with sports-car performance

engineYears.h2: Renault 19 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Renault 19's UK production timeline (1988-1996), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Renault 19 launched with OHV Cléon-Fonte and early SOHC F-series engines across base and mid-trim variants.
keyChanges: UK market launch February 1989 with right-hand drive hatchback
Base RN/RT models used 1.2L C1G and 1.4L C3J Cléon-Fonte engines
Mid-trim TS/GTX introduced 1.7L F2N/F3N carburettor engines
Early production suffered head gasket issues on Cléon-Fonte blocks
knownFor: Cléon-Fonte head gasket vulnerability on early blocks
Carburettor tuning complexity on F2N engines
Strong parts availability for common codes like C3J
Phase 1 dash and trim distinct from later models
years.cta: Get quotes for 1988-1990 Renault 19 replacement engines
years.ticker: Renault 19 1989 head gasket • Renault 19 C3J engine replacement • Renault 19 F2N carburettor repair

years.description: The 1991 facelift introduced fuel injection across more variants and the high-performance F7P 16V engine for 16S models.
keyChanges: Fuel injection (SPI) standardised on 1.4L and 1.7L engines
F7P 16-valve performance engine launched for 16S variant
Chamade saloon body style expanded engine options
Turbo diesel (F8Q) introduced for 1.9 TD models
knownFor: F7P 16V rewards meticulous maintenance with 140hp performance
E7J injection improved cold starts over carburettor predecessors
F8Q turbo diesel offered strong torque for high-mileage driving
Timing belt criticality emphasised for all interference engines
years.cta: Get quotes for 1991-1992 Renault 19 replacement engines
years.ticker: Renault 19 1991 E7J head gasket • Renault 19 F7P timing belt • Renault 19 F8Q injection pump

years.preview: Phase 2 Maturity: Refined Engines & Luxury Trims
years.description: Phase 2 models featured refined F3P 1.8L engines for luxury trims and updated electronics across the range.
keyChanges: F3P 1.8L engine introduced for Baccara and Executive luxury trims
Updated engine management electronics across fuel-injected variants
Cabriolet body style offered with 1.7L and 1.8L 16V engines
Final UK production year 1995 with run-out models into 1996
knownFor: F3P offered smoother power delivery for luxury-oriented buyers
Phase 2 electronics improved reliability but added diagnostic complexity
Cabriolet 16V models command premium in enthusiast markets
Final production models benefit from accumulated engineering refinements
years.cta: Get quotes for 1993-1995 Renault 19 replacement engines
years.ticker: Renault 19 1993 F3P head gasket • Renault 19 Baccara engine replacement • Renault 19 Cabriolet 16V rebuild

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Renault 19 was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Renault 19 specialists.

faq.h2: Renault 19 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Renault 19 engine?
items.answer: For a well-maintained Renault 19 with sentimental or collector value, engine replacement can extend life cost-effectively. However, for high-mileage base models, compare replacement cost against vehicle value before proceeding.
keyPoints: Rebuilt E7J/F3N engines cost £900-£1,700 supply-only
Supply & fit adds £400-£800 labour depending on location
Consider vehicle condition, mileage and emotional value
Rebuilt units include 12-month warranty for peace of mind
items.cta: Compare Renault 19 engine replacement costs vs vehicle value

items.question: How much does a Renault 19 engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Typical UK rebuilt engine prices range from £900 for 1.4L petrol to £2,800 for 1.8L 16V performance units, supply-only. Supply & fit services add £400-£800 labour depending on workshop location.
keyPoints: 1.4L E7J rebuilt: £900-£1,400 supply-only
1.7L F3N rebuilt: £1,100-£1,700 supply-only
1.8L F7P 16V rebuilt: £1,800-£2,800 supply-only
1.9L F8Q diesel rebuilt: £1,200-£1,900 supply-only
items.cta: Get tailored Renault 19 engine quotes from UK specialists

items.question: Will a Renault 19 engine fit a different Renault 19 variant?
items.answer: Engine compatibility depends on engine code, chassis generation (B21/B22) and transmission type. Always verify engine code and mounting points before purchasing a replacement unit.
keyPoints: Phase 1 (B21) and Phase 2 (B22) have different mounts/wiring
Carburettor to fuel injection swaps require ECU/harness changes
Diesel F8Q engines share mounting across D/TD variants
Performance F7P 16V requires specific transmission and ECU
items.cta: Verify Renault 19 engine compatibility by registration lookup

items.question: What engines came in a Renault 19?
items.answer: UK Renault 19s used 1.2L-1.8L petrol engines (C1G, C3J, E7J, F2N, F3N, F3P, F7P) and 1.9L diesel (F8Q) across hatchback, saloon and convertible body styles from 1988-1996.
keyPoints: Petrol: 1.2L C1G/E7F, 1.4L C3J/E7J, 1.6L C2L/C3L, 1.7L F2N/F3N, 1.8L F3P/F7P
Diesel: 1.9L F8Q naturally aspirated and turbo
Performance: F7P 1.8L 16-valve for 16S/16V models
Engine codes vary by year, trim and fuel system type
items.cta: Find your Renault 19 engine code via DVLA registration check

items.question: How long does a reconditioned Renault 19 engine last?
items.answer: A properly reconditioned Renault 19 engine with upgraded components and regular maintenance can last 80,000-120,000 miles. Adherence to service intervals is critical for longevity.
keyPoints: Reconditioned units include new gaskets, seals and timing components
12-month unlimited mileage warranty covers manufacturing defects
Regular oil changes and cooling system maintenance essential
Avoid overheating to prevent repeat head gasket failure
items.cta: View reconditioned Renault 19 engines with warranty coverage

items.question: Can I upgrade my Renault 19 engine?
items.answer: Engine upgrades are possible but require careful planning for ECU, wiring and transmission compatibility. Popular swaps include F3P 1.8L into 1.7L cars or F7P 16V for performance builds.
keyPoints: F3P 1.8L offers bolt-in upgrade path for F3N 1.7L chassis
F7P 16V swaps require transmission, ECU and wiring modifications
Diesel to petrol conversions are complex and rarely cost-effective
Always verify legal compliance and insurance implications
items.cta: Enquire about Renault 19 performance engine upgrade options

items.question: Why do Renault 19 engines fail?
items.answer: Common failure causes include head gasket breach from overheating, timing belt neglect causing valve damage, and injection pump wear on diesel F8Q units. Preventative maintenance is key.
keyPoints: Head gasket failure linked to coolant neglect or block warping
Timing belt must be replaced every 60k miles on interference engines
F8Q diesel injection pumps wear internally after 120k miles
Oil leaks from valve seals or rear main seal increase consumption
items.cta: Diagnose Renault 19 engine problems with specialist support

items.question: Where to find a Renault 19 engine number?
items.answer: Engine codes are stamped on the block near the gearbox flange or on a plate on the cam cover. Clean the area and use a torch to read the code for accurate replacement matching.
keyPoints: Common locations: gearbox flange, cam cover plate, firewall sticker
Codes like E7J601 or F8Q740 identify exact engine specification
DVLA registration lookup can confirm engine code remotely
Photograph the stamp before purchasing a replacement unit
items.cta: Identify your Renault 19 engine code for accurate quotes

items.question: Are used Renault 19 engines reliable?
items.answer: Used Renault 19 engines carry higher risk than rebuilt units due to unknown service history and potential hidden wear. Always request compression tests and mileage verification before purchase.
keyPoints: Used engines cost £400-£1,200 but may have hidden wear
Request compression test report and service history documentation
Rebuilt units offer warranty protection and upgraded components
Consider total cost of ownership including potential repeat repairs
items.cta: Compare used vs rebuilt Renault 19 engine options

items.question: Do you fit Renault 19 engines?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK specialists offering supply & fit services for Renault 19 engines. Labour costs typically add £400-£800 to the engine price depending on location.
keyPoints: Supply & fit includes installation, fluids, ECU reset and road test
Nationwide network of Renault 19-experienced workshops
Warranty registration handled by supplying specialist
Get multiple quotes to compare labour rates and turnaround times
items.cta: Find Renault 19 engine supply & fit deals near you
